question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getBucketName()] Can an unprivileged external user influence the `network` value passed to `ImporterProperties.HederaNetwork.getBlockStreamBucketName()` via environment variable injection or misconfigured externalized config, causing `getBucketName()` to resolve to an attacker-controlled bucket name and redirect block stream ingestion to a malicious source?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getBucketName()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a blank `bucketName` property (e.g., via a public config endpoint or environment override) so that `StringUtils.isNotBlank(bucketName)` returns false, forcing fallback to a network-derived bucket name that resolves to an empty string, causing the importer to silently ingest no blocks and stall the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getBucketName()] Can an unprivileged external user set `bucketName` to a whitespace-only string (e.g., `'   '`) so that `StringUtils.isNotBlank()` returns false, bypassing the explicit bucket override and causing the system to fall back to a default bucket that the attacker has pre-positioned data in?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getBucketName()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of any sanitization on `bucketName` to inject path-traversal sequences (e.g., `../../malicious-bucket`) that, when passed to the cloud storage client, redirect downloads to an unintended bucket or path?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getBucketName()] Can an unprivileged external user set `bucketName` to a value containing null bytes or special characters that are not validated by any `@Pattern` or `@NotBlank` constraint, causing downstream cloud SDK calls to throw uncaught exceptions and crash the importer?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Field: autoDiscoveryEnabled] Can an unprivileged external user flip `autoDiscoveryEnabled` to `true` via an externalized config source, causing `BlockNodeDiscoveryService.discover()` to query the database for registered nodes and expose internal node topology to timing-based side-channel attacks?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Field: autoDiscoveryEnabled] Can an unprivileged external user disable `autoDiscoveryEnabled` (set to `false`) via a config override, causing `BlockNodeDiscoveryService.discover()` to return an empty list and effectively denying block ingestion by removing all auto-discovered nodes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Field: nodes] Can an unprivileged external user inject an arbitrarily large `nodes` collection via externalized configuration (e.g., YAML array with thousands of entries), causing the importer to exhaust memory or CPU during `Collections.sort(result)` in `BlockNodeDiscoveryService.getBlockNodes()`?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Field: nodes] Can an unprivileged external user supply a `nodes` list with duplicate entries having the same `getMergeKey()` value, causing the `HashMap` in `BlockNodeDiscoveryService.getBlockNodes()` to silently drop legitimate nodes and reduce the effective node pool to a single attacker-controlled endpoint?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Field: nodes] Can an unprivileged external user provide a `nodes` collection with no `@Size` upper bound constraint, injecting millions of `BlockNodeProperties` entries to trigger an out-of-memory condition in the importer JVM?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Field: frequency] Can an unprivileged external user set `frequency` to `Duration.ofNanos(1)` (no minimum duration constraint is enforced beyond `@NotNull`), causing the block polling loop to spin at near-zero intervals and exhaust CPU resources in a denial-of-service attack?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Field: frequency] Can an unprivileged external user set `frequency` to an extremely large `Duration` (e.g., `Duration.ofDays(365)`), causing the importer to effectively stop polling for new blocks and stall the mirror node indefinitely?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Field: frequency] Can an unprivileged external user set `frequency` to a negative `Duration` value (no `@DurationMin` constraint is present), causing undefined behavior in the scheduler that uses this value and potentially crashing the importer?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Field: persistBytes] Can an unprivileged external user enable `persistBytes` via externalized config, causing the importer to write raw block bytes to disk for every ingested block, leading to disk exhaustion and a denial-of-service condition?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Field: writeFiles] Can an unprivileged external user enable `writeFiles` via externalized config, causing the importer to write block stream files to the local filesystem, exhausting disk space and causing a denial-of-service?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Field: writeFiles] Can an unprivileged external user enable both `writeFiles` and `persistBytes` simultaneously, doubling the disk write rate and accelerating disk exhaustion compared to enabling either flag alone?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Field: sourceType] Can an unprivileged external user set `sourceType` to `BlockSourceType.AUTO` (the default) and then manipulate the auto-discovery mechanism to inject a malicious block node, causing the importer to ingest forged block data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Field: sourceType] Can an unprivileged external user switch `sourceType` between `BLOCK_NODE` and `FILE` via config override, causing the importer to switch data sources mid-operation and introduce block sequence gaps or duplicate processing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Field: ledger] Can an unprivileged external user supply a null `ledger` property (it has no `@NotNull` constraint), causing a `NullPointerException` in any code path that calls `ledger.toLedger()` or accesses `ledger.getHistoryProofVerificationKey()` without null-checking?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Field: ledger] Can an unprivileged external user provide a `LedgerProperties` with a crafted `historyProofVerificationKey` byte array of zero length (bypassing `@NotEmpty` via a single-byte array), causing cryptographic verification to silently accept any block signature?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Field: ledger] Can an unprivileged external user inject an oversized `historyProofVerificationKey` byte array (no `@Size(max=...)` constraint), causing the importer to allocate excessive heap memory during `LedgerProperties.toLedger()` and trigger an out-of-memory error?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Field: ledger] Can an unprivileged external user supply a `ledgerId` byte array that does not correspond to any known ledger, causing `TssVerifier` to accept blocks from an unrecognized ledger if the verification logic does not strictly validate the ledger ID?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Field: stream] Can an unprivileged external user set `stream.maxBlockItems` to its minimum allowed value of `1000` (via `@Min(1000)`), causing the importer to process only 1000 items per block stream response and artificially throttle ingestion throughput?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Field: stream] Can an unprivileged external user set `stream.maxBlockItems` to `Integer.MAX_VALUE` (no upper bound constraint), causing the importer to attempt to buffer an unbounded number of block items in memory and trigger an out-of-memory condition?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Field: stream] Can an unprivileged external user set `stream.maxStreamResponseSize` to an extremely large `DataSize` value (no `@Max` constraint), causing the importer to allocate a massive buffer for each stream response and exhaust heap memory?
